items.title: apt Module
items.text: The rconf apt module allows you to manage packages on Debian/Ubuntu-based systems. It provides functionalities to update the package cache, install, remove, purge, and manage packages.

items.title: Available Actions
items.text: Each action is executed via the rconf:apt: key in a playbook job, with a run parameter specifying the action and a package parameter if necessary.

items.title: update
items.text:
Updates the list of available packages from the repositories.

Example: 

- name: "Update package cache"
  rconf:apt:
    run: "update"

items.title: upgrade
items.text:
Upgrades all installed packages to their latest versions.

Exemple: 

- name: "Upgrade the packages"
  rconf:apt:
    run: "upgrade"

items.title: full-upgrade
items.text:
Performs a full system upgrade, handling dependency changes.

Exemple: 

- name: "Complete system upgrade"
  rconf:apt:
    run: "full-upgrade"

items.title: install
items.text:
Installs one or more packages.

Exemple: 

- name: "Install Nginx"
  rconf:apt:
    run: "install"
    package: "nginx"

Example (install multiple packages): 

- name: "Install Nginx, Git and Chrony"
  rconf:apt:
    run: "install"
    package: "nginx git chrony"

items.title: remove
items.text:
Removes one or more packages, but keeps configuration files.

Exemple: 

- name: "Remove Nginx"
  rconf:apt:
    run: "remove"
    package: "nginx"

items.title: purge
items.text:
Removes one or more packages along with their configuration files.

Exemple: 

- name: "Purge Nginx"
  rconf:apt:
    run: "purge"
    package: "nginx"

items.title: reinstall
items.text:
Reinstalls an existing package.

Exemple: 

- name: "Reinstall Apache2"
  rconf:apt:
    run: "reinstall"
    package: "apache2"

items.title: autoremove
items.text:
Removes packages that were automatically installed to satisfy dependencies and are no longer needed.

Exemple: 

- name: "Clean up unused dependencies"
  rconf:apt:
    run: "autoremove"

items.title: clean
items.text:
Cleans the cache of downloaded packages (.deb).

Exemple: 

- name: "Clean the APT cache"
  rconf:apt:
    run: "clean"

items.title: autoclean
items.text:
Cleans the cache of downloaded packages, but only removes packages that can no longer be downloaded and are therefore useless.

Exemple: 

- name: "Clear the APT cache (automatic)"
  rconf:apt:
    run: "autoclean"

items.title: list
items.text:
Lists packages. Can be filtered by a package name.

Example (list all packages): 

- name: "List all packages"
  rconf:apt:
    run: "list"

# Example (list a specific package):

- name: "List Nginx package"
  rconf:apt:
    run: "list"
    package: "nginx"

items.title: search
items.text:
Searches for a package by its name or description.

Exemple: 

- name: "Search for a package"
  rconf:apt:
    run: "search"
    package: "php"

items.title: show
items.text:
Displays details of a package.

Exemple: 

- name: "Show Nginx details"
  rconf:apt:
    run: "show"
    package: "nginx"

items.title: satisfy
items.text:
Attempts to satisfy complex dependencies.

Exemple: 

- name: "Satisfying an dependencies"
  rconf:apt:
    run: "satisfy"
    package: "libssl-dev (>= 1.1.1)"

items.title: edit-sources
items.text:
Opens the APT sources configuration file in a text editor. Warning: This command is interactive and may block execution if used in a non-interactive context.

Exemple: 

- name: "Edit APT source code"
  rconf:apt:
    run: "edit-sources"
